name: Tasseography ob3ect: tea leaf reading — loose tea leaves swirled in a cup, then inverted onto a saucer, leaving patterns on the cup walls. FSPLIT: the inverting of the cup splits the wet leaves into scattered clusters and trails across the cup's interior surface. FFUSE: the reader synthesizes the scattered leaf formations into a coherent narrative — the rim=present, middle=near future, bottom=distant future. VINIT: the empty cup. TANCH: the cup's rim as boundary. AFWD: a leaf cluster near the rim (imminent event). AREV: a leaf cluster at the bottom (distant, delayed). ENGAGR: a leaf formation that can be read as two contradictory symbols simultaneously (e.g., a bird and a snake from the same cluster).

phase_0.domain_name: Tasseography ob3ect: tea leaf reading — loose tea leaves swirled in a cup, then inverted onto a saucer, leaving patterns on the cup walls. FSPLIT: the inverting of the cup splits the wet leaves into scattered clusters and trails across the cup's interior surface. FFUSE: the reader synthesizes the scattered leaf formations into a coherent narrative — the rim=present, middle=near future, bottom=distant future. VINIT: the empty cup. TANCH: the cup's rim as boundary. AFWD: a leaf cluster near the rim (imminent event). AREV: a leaf cluster at the bottom (distant, delayed). ENGAGR: a leaf formation that can be read as two contradictory symbols simultaneously (e.g., a bird and a snake from the same cluster).

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before tea leaves are added and swirled

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that bounds the reading space and separates present from future

AFWD.justification: forward morphism toward imminent event, near the boundary

AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism toward distant future, away from rim

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential reading from rim to bottom
CLINK.justification: composition of leaf clusters in temporal order

IMSCRIB.justification: identity/self-recognition that holds the reading together

FSPLIT.justification: splits the wet leaves into scattered clusters and trails across cup interior

FFUSE.chosen_element: reader synthesizing scattered formations into narrative
FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the scattered clusters into a coherent story, matching the original swirl pattern

ENGAGR.chosen_element: leaf formation read as two contradictory symbols
ENGAGR.justification: paradox held simultaneously, e.g., bird and snake from same cluster

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of the reading

phase_2.fuse_element: reader synthesizing scattered formations into narrative
phase_2.fuse_result: coherent story that matches the original swirl pattern

phase_3.both_description: formation read as bird and snake simultaneously

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: reader acknowledges the empty cup as a vessel for meaning
Step 2 AREV: reader inverts the cup onto saucer, letting leaves descend
Step 3 FSPLIT: inversion scatters leaves into clusters and trails
Step 4 AFWD: reader traces leaf clusters from rim inward
Step 5 FFUSE: reader synthesizes clusters into a temporal narrative
Step 6 CLINK: reader links rim (present) to middle (near future) to bottom (distant future)
Step 7 IFIX: reader writes down the interpretation in a journal
Step 8 IMSCRIB: reader closes the session, recognizing the completed reading

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the reader's intuition and symbolic knowledge
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the ritual sequence of swirl, invert, read, record

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the reading cycle preserves the informational content of the leaf patterns as a narrative
